articles.title: He grew up in R.I., named himself after Ted Williams, and is now drawing comparisons to Elvis and Roy Orbison. Meet Tyler Ballgame.
articles.description:
The former Mass. resident and Berklee student is making a splash with his debut album, “For the First Time, Again."

The post He grew up in R.I., named himself after Ted Williams, and is now drawing comparisons to Elvis and Roy Orbison. Meet Tyler Ballgame. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tyler Ballgame, a Berklee student, releases debut album 'For the First Time, Again'.
The Rhode Island native draws comparisons to music legends Elvis and Roy Orbison.
Boston residents can explore new local talent impacting the music scene.

articles.title: For $225k, you could live in America’s oldest shopping mall
articles.description:
The Arcade in Providence is considered America's oldest enclosed shopping mall, and now it houses 48 residences.

The post For $225k, you could live in America’s oldest shopping mall appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Explore living in America's oldest shopping mall, The Arcade, in Providence.
48 residences are available for sale, priced at $225,000 each.
Boston residents can consider a unique living option just a short drive away.

articles.title: Officer improperly canceled visa of Harvard scholar charged with frog embryo smuggling, judge rules
articles.description:
“The undisputed facts reveal that Ms. Petrova’s visa was impermissibly canceled because of the frog embryo samples and for no other reason,” the judge wrote.

The post Officer improperly canceled visa of Harvard scholar charged with frog embryo smuggling, judge rules appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Judge ruled that officer improperly canceled Harvard scholar Petrova's visa over frog embryo smuggling charges.
Petrova's visa cancellation was solely based on the smuggling incident, according to the judge's findings.
The ruling may affect visa protocols for scholars and researchers in Boston.

articles.title: Jayson Tatum is ‘not thrilled’ about first trip to Madison Square Garden since injury
articles.description:
"Last time I played there, obviously it was a traumatic experience for me."

The post Jayson Tatum is ‘not thrilled’ about first trip to Madison Square Garden since injury appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jayson Tatum expresses discomfort about returning to Madison Square Garden after his injury.
Tatum's last game at the venue was traumatic, affecting his mental readiness.
Celtics fans hope Tatum's performance won't be impacted by past experiences.

articles.title: 5 Bruins thoughts on a winless road trip, James Hagens, and playoff matchups
articles.description:
The Bruins could clinch a playoff berth as early as Thursday, but need help from a few teams.

The post 5 Bruins thoughts on a winless road trip, James Hagens, and playoff matchups appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bruins aim to clinch playoff berth by Thursday with external help needed.
James Hagens' performance remains crucial as Bruins face playoff matchups.
Winless road trip raises concerns among Boston fans about team momentum.

articles.title: When she was 11, Vanna Howard was in a refugee camp. Now she’s the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S.
articles.description:
The Lowell resident says she’s focusing on housing, health care, and immigrant communities, while listening closely to the constituents who first recruited her to run.

The post When she was 11, Vanna Howard was in a refugee camp. Now she’s the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Vanna Howard becomes the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S.
Howard, a Lowell resident, prioritizes housing, health care, and immigrant communities.
She aims to address local issues by engaging with constituents who supported her campaign.
